data.description:
# P4 — Sanction settings-side `normalizeAgentIdentity` callers, or refactor

## Why this is P4

Five `normalizeAgentIdentity` calls live in the settings registration
and hydration path. These are arguably legitimate — they canonicalise
agent config at WRITE time (when an agent is registered, scanned, or
hydrated from `settings.toml`) so downstream readers can consume
already-canonical data without re-deriving.

But they currently make the umbrella's self-audit grep noisy, which
makes it impossible to spot REAL violations (the kind in the P0 sibling
knot). This is housekeeping: either formally sanction them in the
contract doc, or refactor so the canonicalisation happens once and is
persisted.

P4 because it's not causing the visible bug — it's making the audit
hard to reason about.

## Sites in scope (5, all in settings / config layer)

| File | Line | Context |
|---|---|---|
| `src/lib/settings.ts` | 232 | Settings hydration on read |
| `src/lib/settings-agent-detect.ts` | 288, 310 | Auto-detection / autoregister of agents |
| `src/lib/settings-agent-scan.ts` | 139, 216 | CLI scan (`opencode models`, `copilot help config`) |
| `src/lib/settings-agent-targets.ts` | 20 | Resolving agent targets for dispatch pools |
| `src/lib/agent-config-normalization.ts` | 108 | `hydrateRegisteredAgentConfig` |

## Two viable paths

### Path A (cheap): Update the contract doc

Add a "Sanctioned exceptions" subsection to
`docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` § "Canonical Functions" that
lists exactly these 5 file paths as the registration / hydration
boundary, and explains the rule:

> The settings layer canonicalises agent config at write time so
> downstream readers consume already-canonical data without
> re-deriving. The five files in this list are the only sites
> outside `agent-identity.ts` and lease creation that may call
> `normalizeAgentIdentity`. New callers MUST be either added to this
> list with justification, or removed.

Update the self-audit grep #2 in the same doc to filter these paths
by name. After the P0 sibling knot ships, the filtered grep returns
zero hits and the umbrella's AC-6 passes.

### Path B (proper): Refactor to canonicalise once

The "right" fix is to canonicalise at the EDGE — when an agent is
registered or scanned — and persist the canonical form to
`settings.toml` so reads don't re-derive. Then:

- `settings.ts:232` (hydration on read) — drops the call; the data
  IS canonical on disk
- `settings-agent-scan.ts` — keeps one call at the very top of the
  scan (write-side); subsequent reads of scan results are pure
- `settings-agent-detect.ts` — same: one canonical call at detect
  time, persist, downstream reads are pure
- `agent-config-normalization.ts:hydrateRegisteredAgentConfig` —
  becomes a no-op once data is canonical at rest, can be removed

This is bigger work but gives the contract teeth: the only sites
that ever call `normalizeAgentIdentity` are agent-identity.ts itself,
the lease creation path, and the registration write-side. Everything
else is a read.

The planner picks A or B in the planning phase.

## Cross-references

- Parent: foolery-2e97 (canonical agent metadata umbrella)
- Sibling P0: addresses the dispatch-side violations (the urgent fix)
- Contract: `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md`
- Tag: `agent-normalizer-fucker` — finds all related knots
